issues.code:
16     with open(config_file, 'rb') as f:

issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

issues.code:
28     cmd = ['docker', 'build', '--file', config['Dockerfile'], '--build-arg', *[f'{k}={v}' for k, v in env.items()], artifact_path]
29     result = run(cmd, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE)
30
issues.description: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.
Pickle deserialization of untrusted data can lead to code execution; avoid pickle for untrusted inputs.

issues.code:
16     with open(config_file, 'rb') as f:
17         config = pickle.load(f)
18
issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
issues.judge_reason: pickle.load() on untrusted file (config_file) allows arbitrary code execution. No sanitization or safe alternative is used.

issues.code:
28     cmd = ['docker', 'build', '--file', config['Dockerfile'], '--build-arg', *[f'{k}={v}' for k, v in env.items()], artifact_path]
29     result = run(cmd, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE)
30
issues.description: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.
issues.judge_reason: subprocess.run() is called with a command list built from untrusted inputs: config['Dockerfile'], env items (from pickle), and artifact_path. No shell=False flag issue, but the list elements contain attacker-controlled data allowing command injection.
